title: OpenClaw exec wrapper trust plan treated side-effecting script/time wrappers as transparent
description: DISPATCH_WRAPPER_SPECS in src/infra/dispatch-wrapper-resolution.ts declares per-wrapper unwrap and transparentUsage behavior used by the exec allowlist trust plan. A wrapper marked transparentUsage: true is unwrapped so that only the inner command is evaluated against the exec allowlist, on the assumption that the wrapper itself has no security-relevant side effects. In the vulnerable release the `script` wrapper and the `time` wrapper were marked transparentUsage: true unconditionally, but both can have file-writing side effects (script records a typescript/output file, and `time -o`/`--output` writes timing output to a file). As a result a command request reaching the exec allowlist path was evaluated only against the inner command while the wrapper invocation (and its file-writing side effects) still executed, performing wrapper-level effects outside the intent of the allowlisted command.
